agents.config.title: Title
agents.config.capabilitiesPlaceholder: Describe what this agent can do...
agents.config.promptTemplatePlaceholder: You are agent {{ agent.name }}. Your role is {{ agent.role }}...
agents.config.promptTemplateHint: Prompt template is replayed on every heartbeat. Keep it compact and dynamic to avoid recurring token cost and cache churn.
agents.config.defaultEnvironmentHint: Agent-level default execution target. Project and issue settings can still override this.
agents.config.noModelDetectedHint: No model detected. Select or enter one manually.
agents.config.noModelDetectedYet: No model detected yet. Enter a provider/model manually.
agents.config.cheapModelDescription: Used when a run requests the cheap profile (e.g. routine summaries). The primary model stays unchanged.
agents.config.noAdapterDefaultHint: No adapter default — choose a cheaper model
agents.config.noExplicitCheapModel: No explicit cheap model selected — runtime falls back to <1>{{model}}</1>.
agents.config.noCheapModelWarning: No cheap model selected and the adapter has no default. Cheap-lane runs will continue on the primary model with a fallback note.
agents.config.codexMinimalThinkingWarning: Codex may reject `minimal` thinking when search is enabled.
agents.config.bootstrapPromptPlaceholder: Optional initial setup prompt for the first run
agents.config.bootstrapPromptWarning: Bootstrap prompt is legacy and will be removed in a future release. Consider moving this content into the agent's prompt template or instructions file instead.

issues.title: Issues
issues.selectCompany: Select a company to view issues.

chat.pausedAgent: {{agentName}} is paused. New runs will not start until the agent is resumed.
chat.pausedBudget: It was paused by a budget hard stop.
chat.pausedSystem: It was paused by the system.
chat.fallbackError: Chat renderer hit an internal state error.
chat.fallbackSafe: Showing a safe fallback transcript instead of crashing the issues page.
chat.feedbackSharingDesc: Choose whether voted AI outputs can be shared with Paperclip Labs. This answer becomes the default for future thumbs up and thumbs down votes.
chat.voteSavedLocally: This vote is always saved locally.
chat.alwaysAllowToShareMiddle: to share this vote and future voted AI outputs. Choose
chat.alwaysAllowToShareAfter: to keep this vote and future votes local.
chat.changeLaterInSettings: You can change this later in Instance Settings > General.
chat.dropToUploadDesc: Images insert into the reply. Other files are added to this issue.
chat.emptyConversation: This issue conversation is empty. Start with a message below.
chat.pickAssigneeOrSend: Pick an assignee or click Send again to post without one.

issues.chooseColumns: Choose which issue columns stay visible
issues.searchResultLimit: Showing up to {{limit}} matches. Refine the search to narrow further.
issues.boardColumnLimit: Some board columns are showing up to {{limit}} issues. Refine filters or search to reveal the rest.
issues.noMatchingIssues: No issues match the current filters or search.
issues.columnDescStatus: Issue state chip on the left edge.
issues.columnDescProject: Linked project pill with its color.
issues.columnDescWorkspace: Execution or project workspace used for the issue.
issues.executionWorkspaceDesc: Control whether this issue runs in the shared workspace, a new isolated workspace, or an existing one.
issues.parentWorkspaceWarning: Warning: this sub-issue will no longer use the parent issue workspace
issues.failedToCreate: Failed to create issue. Try again.
issues.uploadWarningsBody: {{count}} staged file(s) could not be added.
issues.lanePrimaryDesc: Runs on the agent's primary model.
issues.laneCustomDesc: Override the model and effort for this issue only.
issues.label: Label
issues.rejectReason: Add a short reason for rejecting this suggestion
issues.resolutionCreatedWithSkipped: Created {{created}} draft issues and skipped {{skipped}} during review.
issues.draftsSkippedOnAccept: {{count}} will be skipped if you accept this interaction.
issues.submitOnceHint: Submit once after you finish the full form.
issues.expiredByCommentDesc: A board comment superseded this confirmation before it was resolved.
issues.expiredByTargetChangeDesc: The requested target changed before this confirmation was resolved.
issues.requestCouldNotBeResolved: This request could not be resolved. Try again or create a new request.
issues.declineReasonApprove: Optional: what would you like revised?
issues.declineReasonDefault: Optional: tell the agent what you'd change.

projects.title: Projects
projects.selectCompany: Select a company to view projects.

workspace.setupCommandHint: Runs when this workspace needs custom bootstrap
workspace.cleanupCommandHint: Runs before project-level execution workspace teardown
workspace.advancedRuntimeJsonDesc: Paperclip derives Services and Jobs from this JSON. Prefer editing named commands first; use raw JSON for advanced lifecycle, port, readiness, or environment settings.
workspace.workspaceCommandsJsonHint: Execution workspaces inherit this config unless they override it. Legacy `services` arrays still work, but `commands` supports both services and jobs.
workspace.servicesAndJobsDesc: Long-running services stay supervised here, while one-shot jobs run on demand against this workspace. Execution workspaces inherit this config unless they override it.
workspace.primaryWorkspaceDesc: This is the project's primary codebase workspace.
workspace.notFound: Workspace not found for this project.
workspace.workspaceServiceStopped: Workspace service stopped. Issue execution is not paused.
workspace.workspaceServiceRestarted: Workspace service restarted. Issue execution is not paused.
workspace.noServicesStarted: No services have been started for this workspace yet.
workspace.noWorkspaceConfig: No workspace command config is defined for this workspace yet.
workspace.noJobsConfigured: No one-shot jobs are configured for this workspace yet.
workspace.disabledHint: Project workspaces need a working directory before local commands can run, and services also need runtime config.

properties.name: Name
properties.codebaseHelp: Repo identifies the source of truth. Local folder is the default place agents write code.
properties.additionalLegacyWorkspaces: Additional legacy workspace records exist on this project. Paperclip is using the primary workspace as the codebase view.
properties.executionWorkspacesHelp: Project-owned defaults for isolated issue checkouts and execution workspace behavior.
properties.enableIsolatedIssueCheckoutsDesc: Let issues choose between the project's primary checkout and an isolated execution workspace.
properties.newIssuesDefaultToIsolatedCheckout: New issues default to isolated checkout
properties.newIssuesDefaultToIsolatedCheckoutDesc: If disabled, new issues stay on the project's primary checkout unless someone opts in.
properties.provisionCommandDesc: Provision runs inside the derived worktree before agent execution. Teardown is stored here for future cleanup flows.
properties.clearLocalFolderFromWorkspace: Clear local folder from this workspace?
properties.localFolderMustBeAbsolutePath: Local folder must be a full absolute path.
properties.repoMustBeValidGithubUrl: Repo must use a valid GitHub or GitHub Enterprise repo URL.
properties.archiveThisProject: Archive this project to hide it from the sidebar and project selectors.
properties.unarchiveThisProject: Unarchive this project to restore it in the sidebar and project selectors.
properties.workspaceEnvDesc: Applied to all runs for issues in this project. Project values override agent env on key conflicts.

goals.selectCompany: Select a company to view goals.

routines.title: Routines
routines.subtitle: Recurring work definitions that materialize into auditable execution issues.
routines.selectCompany: Select a company to view routines.
routines.noRoutinesYet: No routines yet. Use Create routine to define the first recurring workflow.
routines.newRoutineDesc: Define the recurring work first. Default project and agent are optional for draft routines.
routines.advancedSettingsDesc: Keep policy controls secondary to the work definition.
routines.afterCreation: After creation, Paperclip takes you straight to trigger setup.
routines.label: Label
routines.couldNotSaveRoutine: Paperclip could not save the routine.
routines.couldNotStartRoutineRun: Paperclip could not start the routine run.
routines.couldNotUpdateRoutine: Paperclip could not update the routine.
routines.couldNotCreateTrigger: Paperclip could not create the trigger.
routines.routineCadenceSaved: The routine cadence update was saved.
routines.couldNotUpdateTrigger: Paperclip could not update the trigger.
routines.couldNotDeleteTrigger: Paperclip could not delete the trigger.
routines.couldNotRotateWebhookSecret: Paperclip could not rotate the webhook secret.
routines.setDefaultAgentBeforeEnabling: Set a default agent before enabling routine automation.
routines.saveWebhookSecretNote: Save this now. Paperclip will not show the secret value again.
routines.defaultAgentRequiredDraft: Default agent required. This routine can stay as a draft and still run manually, but automation stays paused until you assign a default agent.
routines.variablesDescription: Detected from `{{name}}` placeholders in the routine title and instructions.
routines.variablesPromptDescription: Prompt the user for this value before each manual run.
routines.variablesHintText: Use `{{variable_name}}` placeholders in the instructions to prompt for inputs when the routine runs.
routines.variablesDialogDescription: How to prompt for inputs and which variables Paperclip fills in automatically.
routines.variablesCustomSectionDesc: Type `{{variable_name}}` anywhere in the title or instructions. Paperclip detects each placeholder, lists it under Variables, and prompts for a value before each run.
routines.variablesNameRule: Names must start with a letter and may use letters, numbers, and underscores.
routines.variablesTypeRule: Pick a type (text, textarea, number, boolean, select), default value, and whether it is required.
routines.variablesSameNameRule: The same name reused across the title and instructions is treated as one variable.
routines.variablesBuiltinSectionDesc: These are filled in automatically — no setup needed and they will not appear in the Variables list.

costs.title: Costs
costs.subtitle: Inference spend, platform fees, credits, and live quota windows.
costs.selectCompany: Select a company to view costs.
costs.financeLedgerDesc: Account-level charges that do not map to a single inference request.
costs.netDesc: Debit minus credit for the selected period
costs.estimatedDesc: Estimated debits that are not yet invoice-authoritative
costs.selectDateRange: Select a start and end date to load data.
costs.inferenceLedgerDesc: Request-scoped inference spend for the selected period.
costs.byAgentDesc: What each agent consumed in the selected period.
costs.byProjectDesc: Run costs attributed through project-linked issues.
costs.noFinanceEvents: No finance events yet. Add account-level charges once biller invoices or credits land.
costs.budgetControlPlaneDesc: Hard-stop spend limits for agents and projects
costs.activeIncidentsDesc: Open soft or hard threshold crossings
costs.pendingApprovalsDesc: Budget override approvals awaiting board action
costs.noCostEventsInPeriod: No cost events in this period.
costs.noBillableEventsInPeriod: No billable events in this period.
costs.byBillerDesc: Account-level financial events grouped by who charged or credited them.
costs.agentBudgetsDesc: Recurring monthly spend policies for individual agents.
costs.projectBudgetsDesc: Lifetime spend policies for execution-bound projects.
costs.noBudgetPolicies: No budget policies yet. Set agent and project budgets from their detail pages.
costs.resolveHardStops: Resolve hard stops here by raising the budget or explicitly keeping the scope paused.
costs.executionPausedProject: Execution is paused for this project until the budget is raised or the incident is dismissed.
costs.heartbeatsPausedScope: Heartbeats are paused for this scope until the budget is raised or the incident is dismissed.
costs.enterValidAmount: Enter a valid non-negative dollar amount.

workspace.configureDescription: Configure the concrete runtime workspace that Paperclip reuses for this issue flow.
workspace.configureDescriptionFull: These settings stay attached to the execution workspace so future runs can keep local paths, repo refs, provisioning, teardown, and runtime-service behavior in sync with the actual workspace being reused.

runtimeControls.noServicesStarted: No services have been started for this execution workspace yet.
runtimeControls.noWorkspaceCommandConfig: No workspace command config is defined for this execution workspace yet.
runtimeControls.noJobsConfigured: No one-shot jobs are configured for this execution workspace yet.
runtimeControls.needsWorkingDir: Execution workspaces need a working directory before local commands can run, and services also need runtime config.

workspace.workspaceSettingsDescription: Edit the concrete path, repo, branch, provisioning, teardown, and runtime overrides attached to this execution workspace.
workspace.provisionCommandHint: Runs when Paperclip prepares this execution workspace
workspace.teardownCommandHint: Runs when the execution workspace is archived or cleaned up

runtimeConfigInherited.executionOverride: This execution workspace currently overrides the project workspace runtime config.
runtimeConfigInherited.projectInherit: This execution workspace is inheriting the project workspace runtime config.
runtimeConfigInherited.none: No runtime config is currently defined on this execution workspace or its project workspace.

workspace.advancedRuntimeJsonDescription: Override the inherited workspace command model only when this execution workspace truly needs different service or job behavior.

step1.title: Name your company
step1.subtitle: This is the organization your agents will work for.

step2.title: Create your first agent
step2.subtitle: Choose how this agent will run tasks.
step2.adapterCheckDescription: Runs a live probe that asks the adapter CLI to respond with hello.
step2.authFailHint: If auth fails, set {{apiKey}} in env or run {{loginCmd}}.
step2.claudeLoginHint: If login is required, run claude login and retry.
step2.anthropicKeyHint: Claude failed while ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set. You can clear it in this CEO adapter config and retry the probe.

errors.noCompanySelected: Create or select a company before testing adapter environment.
errors.opencodeRequiresModel: OpenCode requires an explicit model in provider/model format.
errors.opencodeModelsLoading: OpenCode models are still loading. Please wait and try again.
errors.noOpencodeModelsDiscovered: No OpenCode models discovered. Run `opencode models` and authenticate providers.
errors.anthropicKeyRetryFailed: Retried with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset in adapter config, but the environment test is still failing.
errors.unsetAnthropicKeyFailed: Failed to unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and retry.

step3.title: Give it something to do

step4.title: Ready to launch
step4.subtitle: Everything is set up. Launching now will create the starter task, wake the agent, and open the issue.

skills.title: Skills
skills.selectCompany: Select a company to manage skills.
skills.selectSkillToInspect: Select a skill to inspect its files.
skills.detachBeforeRemove: Detach this skill from all agents before removing it.
skills.removeSkillDescription: Remove this skill from the company library. If any agents still use it, removal will be blocked until it is detached.
skills.aboutToRemoveThis: You are about to remove this skill.
skills.detachToRemove: Detach this skill from all agents to enable removal.
skills.addSkillSourceDescription: Paste a local path, GitHub URL, or `skills.sh` command into the field first.
skills.findInstallCommands: Find install commands and paste one here.
skills.lookForRepositories: Look for repositories with `SKILL.md`, then paste the repo URL here.
skills.pasteSourcePlaceholder: Paste path, GitHub URL, or skills.sh command
skills.skillCreatedBody: {{name}} is now editable in the Paperclip workspace.
skills.removedFromLibrary: {{name}} was removed from the company skill library.

company.noCompanySelected: No company selected. Select a company from the switcher above.

exportPage.noPreview: Select a file to preview its contents.
exportPage.binaryPreview: Binary asset preview is not available for this file type.

env.selectCompany: Select a company to manage environments.
env.title: Company Environments
env.environmentsDisabled: Enable Environments in instance experimental settings to manage company execution targets.
env.pageDescription: Define reusable execution targets for projects, issue workspaces, and remote-capable adapters.
env.adapterSupportNote: Environment choices use the same adapter support matrix as agent defaults. SSH is always available for remote-managed adapters, and sandbox environments appear only when a run-capable sandbox provider plugin is installed.
env.noEnvironmentsYet: No environments saved for this company yet.
env.nameHint: Operator-facing name for this execution target.
env.descriptionHint: Optional note about what this machine is for.
env.driverHint: Local runs on this host. SSH stores a remote machine target. Sandbox stores plugin-backed provider config on the shared environment seam.
env.hostHint: DNS name or IP address for the remote machine.
env.remoteWorkspacePathHint: Absolute path that Paperclip will verify during SSH connection tests.
env.privateKeyHint: Optional PEM private key. Leave blank to rely on the server's SSH agent or default keychain.
env.knownHostsHint: Optional known_hosts block used when strict host key checking is enabled.
env.strictHostKeyCheckingHint: Keep this on unless you deliberately want probe-time host key acceptance disabled.
env.providerHint: Installed run-capable sandbox provider plugins appear here.
env.noAdditionalConfig: This provider does not declare additional configuration fields.

accessPage.title: Company Access
accessPage.subtitle: Manage company user memberships, membership status, and explicit permission grants for {{name}}.
accessPage.selectCompany: Select a company to manage access.
accessPage.instanceAdminMessage: This account can manage access here through instance-admin privileges, but it does not currently hold an active company membership.
accessPage.errorNoPermission: You do not have permission to manage company members.
accessPage.manageHumanMemberships: Manage human company memberships, status, and grants here.
accessPage.pendingHumanJoinsDesc: Review human join requests before they become active company members.
accessPage.noMemberships: No user memberships found for this company yet.

dialog.editDesc: Update company role, membership status, and explicit grants for {{name}}.
dialog.grantsDesc: Roles provide implicit grants automatically. Explicit grants below are only for overrides and extra access that should persist even if the role changes.
dialog.noImplicitGrants: No role is selected, so this member has no implicit grants right now.
dialog.implicitGrantHint: Included implicitly by the {{role}} role. Add an explicit grant only if it should stay after the role changes.
dialog.removeDesc: Archive {{name}} and move active assignments before hiding this user from assignment fields.

companyInvites.title: Company Invites
companyInvites.description: Create human invite links for company access. New invite links are copied to your clipboard when they are generated.
companyInvites.createInviteDescription: Generate a human invite link and choose the default access it should request.

roleDescription.viewer: Can view company work and follow along without operational permissions.
roleDescription.operator: Recommended for people who need to help run work without managing access.
roleDescription.admin: Recommended for operators who need to invite people, create agents, and approve joins.
roleDescription.owner: Full company access, including membership and permission management.

gets.admin: Can create agents, invite users, assign tasks, and approve join requests.
gets.owner: Everything in Admin, plus managing members and permission grants.

companyInvites.singleUseNote: Each invite link is single-use. The first successful use consumes the link and creates or reuses the matching join request before approval.
companyInvites.inviteHistoryDescription: Review invite status, role, inviter, and any linked join request.
companyInvites.noInvites: No invites have been created for this company yet.

adapterManager.title: Adapters
adapterManager.alphaDescription: The adapter plugin system is under active development. APIs and storage format may change. Use the power icon to hide adapters from agent menus without removing them.
adapterManager.noExternalDesc: Install an adapter package to extend model support.
adapterManager.installDesc: Add an adapter from npm or a local path. The adapter package must export createServerAdapter().
adapterManager.pathHint: Accepts Linux, WSL, and Windows paths. Windows paths are auto-converted.
adapterManager.reinstallDesc: This will pull the latest version from npm and hot-swap the running adapter module. Existing agents will use the new version on their next run.
adapterManager.removeDesc: Are you sure you want to remove this adapter? It will be unregistered and removed from the adapter store.
adapterManager.npmCleanup: npm packages will be cleaned up from disk.

instance.title: Scheduler Heartbeats
instance.description: Agents with a timer heartbeat enabled across all of your companies.
instance.disableConfirm: Disable timer heartbeats for all {{count}} enabled {{noun}}?
instance.noHeartbeats: No scheduler heartbeats match the current criteria.

generalSettings.title: General
generalSettings.pageDescription: Configure instance-wide preferences including log display, keyboard shortcuts, backup retention, and data sharing.
generalSettings.localTrustedDescription: Local trusted mode is optimized for a local operator. Browser requests run as local board context and no sign-in is required.
generalSettings.authenticatedPublicDescription: Authenticated public mode requires sign-in for board access and is intended for public URLs.
generalSettings.authenticatedPrivateDescription: Authenticated private mode requires sign-in and is intended for LAN, VPN, or other private-network deployments.
generalSettings.censorUsernameDescription: Hide the username segment in home-directory paths and similar operator-visible log output. Standalone username mentions outside of paths are not yet masked in the live transcript view. This is off by default.
generalSettings.keyboardShortcutsDescription: Enable app keyboard shortcuts, including inbox navigation and global shortcuts like creating issues or toggling panels. This is off by default.
generalSettings.backupRetentionDescription: Configure how long automatic database backups are retained. Backups run roughly every hour and are compressed with gzip. Within the daily window all backups are kept; beyond that, one backup per week and one per month are preserved.
generalSettings.aiFeedbackSharingDescription: Control whether thumbs up and thumbs down votes can send the voted AI output to Paperclip Labs. Votes are always saved locally.
generalSettings.feedbackPromptNoDefault: No default is saved yet. The next thumbs up or thumbs down choice will ask once and then save the answer here.
generalSettings.dontAllowDescription: Keep voted AI outputs local only.
generalSettings.feedbackDevHint: To retest the first-use prompt in local dev, remove the feedbackDataSharingPreference key from the instance_settings.general JSON row for this instance, or set it back to "prompt". Unset and "prompt" both mean no default has been chosen yet.
generalSettings.signOutDescription: Sign out of this Paperclip instance. You will be redirected to the login page.

experimentalPage.title: Experimental
experimentalPage.description: Opt into features that are still being evaluated before they become default behavior.
experimentalPage.enableEnvironmentsDesc: Show environment management in company settings and allow project and agent environment assignment controls.
experimentalPage.enableIsolatedWorkspacesDesc: Show execution workspace controls in project configuration and allow isolated workspace behavior for new and existing issue runs.
experimentalPage.autoRestartDevServerDesc: In `pnpm dev:once`, wait for all queued and running local agent runs to finish, then restart the server automatically when backend changes or migrations make the current boot stale.
experimentalPage.autoCreateRecoveryTasksDesc: Let the heartbeat scheduler create recovery issues for issue dependency chains found inside the configured lookback window.
experimentalPage.lookbackHoursInvalid: Lookback hours must be a whole number from 1 to 720.

recoveryDialog.title: Confirm auto-recovery
recoveryDialog.noRecoveryTasks: No recovery tasks would be created right now. Auto-recovery can still run for future liveness incidents in this window.
recoveryDialog.outsideLookback: {{count}} current {{count === 1 ? 'finding is' : 'findings are'}} outside the configured lookback and will not be touched.

plugins.installDescription: Enter the npm package name of the plugin you wish to install.
plugins.alphaWarningDescription: The plugin runtime and API surface are still changing. Expect breaking changes while this feature settles.
plugins.noBundledExamples: No bundled example plugins were found in this checkout.
plugins.installPluginToExtend: Install a plugin to extend functionality.
plugins.uninstallConfirm: Are you sure you want to uninstall {{name}}? This action cannot be undone.
plugins.author: Author
plugins.configureFromEnvironments: Configure this plugin from Company Environments.
plugins.registersEnvironmentSettings: registers environment runtime settings there so credentials stay company-scoped instead of instance-global.
plugins.noSettingsRequired: This plugin does not require any settings.
plugins.runtimeDashboardDesc: Worker process, scheduled jobs, and webhook deliveries
plugins.runtimeDiagnosticsUnavailable: Runtime diagnostics are unavailable right now.

plugins.multiplePluginsDeclareRoute: Use the plugin-id route until the conflict is resolved.

designGuide.title: Design Guide
designGuide.subtitle: Every component, style, and pattern used across Paperclip.
designGuide.componentCoverageDesc: This page should be updated when new UI primitives or app-level patterns ship.
designGuide.statusIconHint: Click the icon to change status (current: {{status}})
designGuide.issueReferencePillDesc: Used wherever a task is referenced — in markdown, the Related Work tab, and activity summaries. Pass status to show the target issue's state at a glance. Use strikethrough for "removed" contexts.
designGuide.popoverDesc: Last run succeeded 24s ago. Next timer run in 9m.
designGuide.sheetDesc: Edit metadata without leaving the current page.
designGuide.cardContent: Card content goes here. This is the main body area.
designGuide.dialogDesc: This is a sample dialog showing the standard layout with header, content, and footer.

lab.description: Small cards for the fast-return invite states that do not render the full split-screen layout.

lab.statesCompanyInviteCreation: Company invite creation, copied-link, history, empty, and permission-error states

summary.invitedToJoin: You've been invited to join Paperclip

invite.landingDescription: These frames mirror the production invite surface closely enough to review spacing, hierarchy, and control states while keeping data fixture-driven.
invite.joinAcmeRoboticsDesc1: Create your Paperclip account first. If you already have one, switch to sign in and continue the invite with the same email.
invite.joinAcmeRoboticsDesc2: Your account is ready. Review the invite details, then accept it to continue.
invite.joinAcmeRoboticsDesc3: Review the invite details, then submit the agent information below to start the join request.
invite.authDescSignUp: Start with a Paperclip account. After that, you'll come right back here to accept the invite for Acme Robotics.
invite.authDescSignIn: Use the Paperclip account that already matches this invite. If you do not have one yet, switch back to create account.
invite.name: Name
invite.signUpHint: Already signed up before? Use the existing-account option instead so the invite lands on the right Paperclip user.
invite.signInHint: No account yet? Switch back to create account so you can accept the invite with a new login.
invite.submitAgentDetailsDesc: This invite will create an approval request for a new agent in Acme Robotics.
invite.acceptCompanyInviteDesc1: Submitting your join request for Acme Robotics.
invite.acceptCompanyInviteDesc2: This account already belongs to Acme Robotics.
invite.acceptCompanyInviteDesc3: This will submit or complete your join request for Acme Robotics.
invite.approvalPageHint: Ask them to visit Company Settings → Access to approve your request.
invite.refreshPageHint: Refresh this page after you've been approved — you'll be redirected automatically.
invite.boardUserMustApprove: Board User must approve your request to join.
invite.useEmailPassword: Use your email and password to access this instance.
invite.emailConfirmationNotRequired: Create an account for this instance. Email confirmation is not required in v1.
invite.mirrorsProductionAuth: This frame mirrors the production auth surface so spacing, label density, button treatments, and desktop composition are easy to compare.
invite.createInviteDesc: Generate a human invite link and choose the default access it should request.
invite.singleUseNote: Each invite link is single-use. The first successful use consumes the link and creates or reuses the matching join request before approval.
invite.inviteHistoryBelow: Invite history below keeps the audit trail.
invite.latestInviteLinkDesc: This URL includes the current Paperclip domain returned by the server.
invite.inviteHistoryDesc: Review invite status, role, inviter, and any linked join request.
invite.emptyHistoryStateDesc: No invites have been created for this company yet.
invite.permissionErrorDesc: You do not have permission to manage company invites.
invite.labTitle: Invite and signup UX review surface
invite.labDesc: This page collects the current invite landing, signup, approval-result, and company invite-management states in one place so styling changes can be reviewed without recreating each backend condition by hand.
invite.resultDesc: These are the post-submit states returned from invite acceptance, including optional claim and onboarding metadata.
invite.authDesc: The general `/auth` page uses a different composition from invite landing. These previews keep both sign-in and sign-up variants visible.
invite.companySettingsDesc: This section captures the board-side invite creation flow, copied-link state, audit table, and the edge states that are otherwise tedious to stage.
invite.statusLoadingBody: Shown while invite summary, deployment mode, or auth session data is still loading.
invite.statusCheckingAccessBody: Shown after sign-in while the app verifies whether the current user already belongs to the invited company.
invite.statusInvalidTokenBody: The token is missing entirely, so the page short-circuits before any invite lookup.
invite.statusNotAvailableBody: Used for expired, revoked, already-consumed, or otherwise missing invites.
invite.statusBootstrapCompleteBody: Result screen for bootstrap CEO invites after setup has been accepted successfully.
invite.statusAutoAcceptBody: Signed-in human users skip the extra button click and move straight into join submission.
invite.statusAlreadyMemberBody: Acceptance stays disabled and the page redirects into the company once membership is confirmed.
invite.statusResultSurfacesBody: Both pending-approval and joined-now confirmations are included below with claim and onboarding extras.

invite.landing.inviteExpiredOrUsed: This invite may be expired, revoked, or already used.
invite.landing.youBeenInvited: You've been invited to join Paperclip
invite.landing.reviewAgentDetails: Review the invite details, then submit the agent information below to start the join request.
invite.landing.createAccountFirst: Create your Paperclip account first. If you already have one, switch to sign in and continue the invite with the same email.
invite.landing.accountReady: Your account is ready. Review the invite details, then accept it to continue.
invite.landing.submitAgentDetailsDesc: This invite will create an approval request for a new agent in {{company}}.
invite.landing.startWithPaperclip: Start with a Paperclip account. After that, you will come right back here to accept the invite for {{company}}.
invite.landing.useExistingPaperclip: Use the Paperclip account that already matches this invite. If you do not have one yet, switch back to create account.
invite.landing.name: Name
invite.landing.pleaseFillRequired: Please fill in all required fields.
invite.landing.alreadySignedUp: Already signed up before? Use the existing-account option instead so the invite lands on the right Paperclip user.
invite.landing.noAccountYet: No account yet? Switch back to create account so you can accept the invite with a new login.
invite.landing.submitOrCompleteJoin: submit or complete your join request for {{company}}
invite.landing.approveYourRequest: {{name}} must approve your request to join.
invite.landing.approveYourRequestLink: Ask them to visit {{url}} to approve your request.
invite.landing.refreshPageHint: Refresh this page after you've been approved — you'll be redirected automatically.
invite.landing.accountExistsFor: An account already exists for {{email}}. Sign in below to continue with this invite.
invite.landing.emailPasswordMismatch: That email and password did not match an existing Paperclip account. Check both fields, or create an account first if you are new here.
invite.landing.accountMayExist: An account may already exist for {{email}}. Try signing in instead.
invite.landing.checkingCompanyAccess: Checking your company access. Try again in a moment.
invite.landing.accountBelongsCompany: This account already belongs to the company.
invite.landing.thisAccountAlreadyBelongs: This account already belongs to the company.
invite.landing.joinRequestRejected: This join request was not approved.
invite.landing.joinRequestUsed: This invite has already been used.

envVar.createSecretTooltip: Create secret from current plain value
envVar.sealTooltip: Store value as secret and replace with reference
envVar.automaticInjectionHint: PAPERCLIP_* variables are injected automatically at runtime.

routineRun.defaultAgentRequired: Default agent required for this run.
routineRun.chooseExistingWorkspace: Choose an existing workspace before running.
routineRun.description: Choose the agent and optional project for this one run. Routine defaults are prefilled and won't be changed.

workspaceClose.checkingSafety: Checking whether this workspace is safe to close...
workspaceClose.sharedWorkspace: This is a shared workspace session. Archiving it removes this session record but keeps the underlying project workspace.
workspaceClose.ownCheckoutPath: This execution workspace has its own checkout path and can be archived independently.
workspaceClose.projectPrimary: This execution workspace currently points at the project's primary workspace path.
workspaceClose.disposable: This workspace is disposable and can be archived.
workspaceClose.failedToInspect: Failed to inspect workspace close readiness.
workspaceClose.archiveDescription: Archive {{name}} and clean up any owned workspace artifacts. Paperclip keeps the workspace record and issue history, but removes it from active workspace views.
workspaceClose.cleanupFailedRetry: Cleanup previously failed on this workspace. Retrying close will rerun the cleanup flow and update the workspace status if it succeeds.

accessGate.bootstrapInviteActive: A bootstrap invite is already active. Check your Paperclip startup logs for the first admin invite URL, or run this command to rotate it:
accessGate.runBootstrapCommand: Run this command in your Paperclip environment to generate the first admin invite URL:
accessGate.notMemberOrAdmin: This account is signed in, but it does not have an active company membership or instance-admin access on this Paperclip instance.
accessGate.useInviteOrSignIn: Use a company invite or sign in with an account that already belongs to this org.

documentDiff.sameRevision: Both sides are the same revision.

dev.waitingForRuns: Waiting for {{count}} live runs to finish
dev.autoRestartWhenIdle: Auto-restart will trigger when the instance is idle
dev.restartPnpmDevOnce: Restart pnpm dev:once after the active work is safe to interrupt
